pi_items.source: Task description and setup script confirm the plan should be saved as 'VOR_PRACTICE'

Task description and verifier logic; KRHV is Reid-Hillview Airport (https://www.airnav.com/airport/KRHV), KSQL is San Carlos Airport (https://www.airnav.com/airport/KSQL), SJC is San Jose VOR/DME (https://www.airnav.com/cgi-bin/navaid-info?id=SJC&type=VOR.DME&name=SAN+JOSE)
Web search confirms SJC is the identifier for San Jose VOR/DME navigation station operating on 114.10 MHz (https://www.airnav.com/cgi-bin/navaid-info?id=SJC&type=VOR.DME&name=SAN+JOSE)
Web search confirms KSJC is the ICAO identifier for Norman Y Mineta San Jose International Airport (https://www.airnav.com/airport/ksjc)

privileged_info_summary: The plan_vor_route task uses real aviation data. KRHV (Reid-Hillview Airport), KSQL (San Carlos Airport), and KSJC (San Jose International Airport) are actual California airports. SJC is a real VOR/DME navigation station operated at San Jose on 114.10 MHz. The critical distinction between SJC (VOR station) and KSJC (airport) is correctly reflected in the metadata and task design. Avare is a real, open-source Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) application (com.ds.avare) that includes FAA aviation databases with real airport and navigation aid information. The task requires creating a flight plan named 'VOR_PRACTICE' with these three waypoints, emphasizing the correct selection of the VOR station (SJC) rather than the nearby airport (KSJC).
